title: Cloud SQL for MySQL: Qwik Start
aim: To create a Cloud SQL MySQL instance, connect to it via Cloud Shell, and perform basic SQL operations.

steps.title: Create a Cloud SQL Instance
steps.description: Go to SQL > Create Instance. Choose MySQL. Select Enterprise edition. Instance ID: myinstance. Generate and note down the root password. Click Create.

steps.title: Connect Using mysql Client
steps.description: Wait for instance to be ready. In Cloud Shell, run 'gcloud sql connect' command and enter the password.

steps.title: Create Database and Table
steps.description: Inside the MySQL prompt, run SQL commands to create a database 'guestbook', a table 'entries', and insert data.

commands.purpose: Connect to the Cloud SQL instance from Cloud Shell

commands.cmd: CREATE TABLE entries (guestName VARCHAR(255), content VARCHAR(255), entryID INT NOT NULL AUTO_INCREMENT, PRIMARY KEY(entryID));
commands.purpose: Create a new table with an auto-incrementing primary key
Retrieve all data from the table

viva.question: What is Cloud SQL?
viva.answer: It is a fully managed relational database service for MySQL, PostgreSQL, and SQL Server.

viva.question: Why use a managed database like Cloud SQL?
viva.answer: It automates tasks like backups, replication, and patching, allowing developers to focus on applications instead of database administration.

viva.question: What is an instance ID?
viva.answer: A unique identifier for your database instance within a project.

notes: Cloud SQL handles backups, replication, and patching automatically.
